title: Skills & Experience
text: Bridging field science and data — from reef surveys to R scripts

title: Skills & Experience
text:
Professional Experience

Ecological Data Manager
Mwambao Coastal Community Network | Tanzania
Led ecological data management across a network of coastal monitoring sites spanning Tanzania’s coastline. Responsible for designing monitoring protocols, managing field teams, building data pipelines, and producing reports for conservation decision-making.
Key responsibilities: - Biodiversity monitoring programme design and coordination - Spatial data management and GIS mapping (seagrass, mangrove, coral extent) - Statistical analysis of long-term ecological trends in R - Stakeholder reporting — government, NGO, and community audiences - Community training and capacity building (500+ people trained) - Documentary and media engagement as field science expert


MSc Marine Science
University / Institution | Year of Completion
Graduate research focused on [coastal ecology / marine biodiversity / relevant thesis topic]. Developed rigorous scientific methodology in ecological field surveys, statistical analysis, and conservation planning.

title: Laurian Kaijage
text:
This website serves as a portfolio and professional CV showcasing my work in marine science, ecological data management, and coastal conservation.

What You’ll Find Here

Home: Introduction and overview of my professional background
Projects: Detailed descriptions of my recent and ongoing projects in marine conservation and data science
CV: Complete curriculum vitae with education, experience, and skills
About: Information about this site and how to connect with me



Professional Background
I am a marine scientist with a strong passion for understanding and protecting coastal ecosystems. With expertise in ecological data management, statistical analysis, and conservation planning, I work to bridge the gap between scientific research and practical conservation implementation.
My work at Mwambao Coastal Community Network focuses on: - Biodiversity monitoring and assessment - Data-driven conservation planning - Community engagement and capacity building - Ecosystem resilience and climate adaptation


Technical Expertise
I am proficient in R programming, GIS analysis, and data visualization. I use these tools to transform raw ecological data into actionable insights that inform conservation decisions and support sustainable management of coastal resources.


Let’s Connect
I’m always interested in collaborating on conservation projects, discussing marine science, or exploring opportunities to apply data science in environmental contexts. Feel free to reach out through:

Email: lauriankaijage@gmail.com
GitHub: Laurian Kaijage
LinkedIn: Laurian Kaijage




About This Website
This portfolio website was built using Quarto, a modern publishing system for combining code, data, and narrative content. The source code is available on GitHub.

title: Laurian Kaijage
text:
What I Do


🌊 Field Research
Leading biodiversity surveys and ecological assessments across Tanzania’s coastline — from seagrass beds to coral reefs and mangrove forests.


📊 Data & Analysis
Turning field observations into actionable insights using R, GIS, and statistical modelling to guide conservation decisions.


🤝 Community Work
Building local capacity for conservation through training, engagement, and co-designing monitoring programs with coastal fishing communities.


🎬 Media & Advocacy
Participating in documentary productions and science communication to amplify conservation stories from the local coastal communities.

title: Impact & Ground Work
text: From the reef to the community — real outcomes on the Swahili Coast

title: Impact & Ground Work
text:
Work on the Ground
My conservation work spans 50 communities across Zanzibar and the Tanga region of Northern Tanzania. Every protocol I design, every dataset I analyse, and every person I train is part of a connected effort to put ecological evidence at the centre of coastal management decisions.

title: Impact & Ground Work
text:
Key Impact Areas


👥 Community Training — Fisheries Data Collection
Trained 200 community members across 50 communities (Zanzibar and Tanga region) on fisheries data collection protocols covering reef fish, prawns, and octopus. Training equipped local fishers with the skills to systematically record catch and effort data, turning everyday fishing activity into a conservation monitoring tool.
Outcomes: - 200 trained community monitors actively collecting fisheries data - 50 communities covered across Zanzibar and Northern Tanzania - Community-generated data fed directly into local management plans and adaptive management decisions - Increased local ownership of fisheries resource management


🤿 In-Water Monitoring — Coral Reef Visual Census
Trained 300+ in-water monitors in underwater visual census (UVC) techniques for coral reef monitoring, including data collection and analysis. A landmark achievement within this programme: 70 of these monitors are women — breaking a significant cultural barrier in coastal Tanzania, where women’s participation in snorkelling and underwater activities is highly unconventional.
Outcomes: - 300+ trained underwater visual census monitors - 70 women trained as in-water monitors — unprecedented in this region - Training extended beyond data collection to include analysis of survey data - Results used to guide and support coral reef restoration efforts


🪸 Coral Reef Restoration Support
Provided data analysis and monitoring support for the restoration of 347 hectares of coral reef replenishment zones, including the deployment of 1,300 artificial reef structures. This work involved designing data collection protocols, coordinating field surveys, and analysing reef health data to track restoration progress.
Outcomes: - 347 ha of coral reef replenishment zone established and monitored - 1,300 artificial reef structures deployed and tracked - Reef health data used to measure restoration effectiveness - Monitoring protocols adapted for long-term replenishment zone management


🌿 Mangrove Restoration & Seagrass Monitoring
Supported the restoration of approximately 60 hectares of mangrove habitat through data collection protocol design and analysis. Also led seagrass monitoring surveys contributing to understanding of coastal habitat connectivity and ecosystem health.
Outcomes: - 60 ha of mangrove area supported through data-driven restoration - Seagrass monitoring integrated into multi-habitat coastal assessment - Restoration progress tracked with standardised protocols - Data informing coastal ecosystem management decisions


📋 Protocols & Survey Tools
Developed Mwambao’s second fish catch monitoring data protocol — a landmark institutional tool that standardises fisheries data collection across the organisation’s entire network. Created 10+ ecological survey forms covering all major survey types used by the ecological department.
Outcomes: - New fish catch monitoring protocol in active use across the network - 10+ survey forms designed for reef fish, coral, seagrass, mangrove, and octopus monitoring - Standardised data collection reducing errors and improving comparability - Institutional data quality significantly improved


📊 Ecological Data Analysis
Led all ecological data analysis for Mwambao’s ecological department — spanning fisheries, mangrove restoration, seagrass monitoring, and coral reef monitoring. Transforming raw field data from across the network into findings that drive conservation planning and adaptive management.
Outcomes: - End-to-end analysis across four ecosystem monitoring programmes - Annual reports and internal dashboards produced for each programme - Data-driven adaptive management recommendations delivered to teams - Analytical pipelines built in R for reproducible, efficient reporting

title: Impact & Ground Work
text:
Conservation Timeline


  
    
      2019
      Joined Mwambao Coastal Community Network
      Began ecological monitoring fieldwork along Tanzania's northern coast. Established foundational data systems and began engaging coastal communities in conservation activities.
    
  

  
    
      2020
      Community Fisheries Training Launched
      Designed and rolled out fisheries data collection training across coastal communities in Zanzibar and the Tanga region — covering reef fish, prawns, and octopus monitoring protocols.
    
  

  
    
      2021
      In-Water Monitor Training Programme
      Launched a major training programme for 300+ in-water monitors on underwater visual census techniques. Trained 70 women as underwater monitors — a historic first in this region's conservation work.
    
  

  
    
      2022
      Fish Catch Monitoring Protocol & Survey Forms
      Developed Mwambao's second institutional fish catch monitoring protocol and created 10+ standardised ecological survey forms across all ecosystem types monitored by the ecological department.
    
  

  
    
      2023
      Coral Reef Restoration — 347 Ha & 1,300 Artificial Reefs
      Provided data analysis and monitoring support for the establishment of 347 hectares of coral reef replenishment zones, including the deployment and tracking of 1,300 artificial reef structures.
    
  

  
    
      2024
      Mangrove Restoration — 60 Ha Supported
      Supported the restoration of 60 hectares of mangrove habitat through data collection protocol design, field surveys, and analysis — alongside continued seagrass and multi-habitat monitoring.
    
  

  
    
      2025
      Leading All Ecological Data Analysis
      Took full responsibility for all ecological data analysis across Mwambao's department — fisheries, coral reef, mangrove, and seagrass programmes — driving adaptive management and long-term conservation planning.

title: Projects
text: Science in action — research, monitoring, and restoration at scale

title: Projects
text:
Featured Projects


Community Fisheries Training Programme
Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network Reach: Zanzibar & Tanga Region, Northern Tanzania
Designed and delivered fisheries data collection training to 200 community members across 50 coastal communities, covering protocols for reef fish, prawns, and octopus monitoring. Community-generated data is fed into local management plans and used to drive adaptive management decisions — putting fishers at the centre of their own resource management.
Outcomes: - 200 trained community monitors across 50 communities - Reef fish, prawn, and octopus data collection protocols adopted - Community data directly informs local fisheries management plans - Adaptive management decisions supported by community-generated data
Tools: KoboToolbox · Field data forms · R · Training materials
Community Training Fisheries Adaptive Management


In-Water Monitor Training — Underwater Visual Census
Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
Trained 300+ community members as in-water monitors using underwater visual census (UVC) techniques for coral reef monitoring. Training covered both data collection and analysis, enabling monitors to independently assess reef health and contribute to restoration planning. A landmark outcome: 70 women were trained as in-water monitors — highly unconventional in coastal Tanzania where women’s participation in snorkelling is culturally rare.
Outcomes: - 300+ in-water monitors trained in UVC methodology - 70 women trained as underwater monitors — unprecedented in this region - Training extended to data analysis, building full monitoring capacity - UVC data used to guide coral reef restoration and replenishment planning
Tools: Underwater survey slates · KoboToolbox · R · Field data analysis
Coral Reef Monitoring Gender Inclusion UVC Capacity Building


Coral Reef Restoration — Data Support
Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
Provided end-to-end data support for the restoration of 347 hectares of coral reef replenishment zones, including the deployment and monitoring of 1,300 artificial reef structures. Responsibilities covered data collection protocol design, field survey coordination, and analysis of reef health data to measure restoration effectiveness over time.
Outcomes: - 347 ha of coral reef replenishment zone established and monitored - 1,300 artificial reef structures deployed and tracked - Reef health monitoring data informing ongoing restoration management - Monitoring protocols developed for long-term replenishment zone tracking
Tools: Underwater survey forms · R · QGIS · KoboToolbox
Coral Restoration Artificial Reef Data Analysis


Mangrove Restoration — Data & Protocol Support
Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
Supported the restoration of approximately 60 hectares of mangrove habitat through data collection protocol design and ecological analysis. Work included field surveys to track restoration progress, analysis of mangrove extent and health, and integration with seagrass monitoring for a connected coastal habitat picture.
Outcomes: - 60 ha of mangrove area supported through data-driven restoration - Restoration monitoring protocols designed and implemented - Mangrove extent and health tracked using standardised field surveys - Data integrated with seagrass and reef monitoring for holistic coastal assessment
Tools: QGIS · R · Field survey forms · KoboToolbox
Mangrove Restoration Seagrass Habitat Monitoring


Fish Catch Monitoring Protocol Development
Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
Developed Mwambao’s second institutional fish catch monitoring protocol — a comprehensive data collection framework standardising how fisheries catch and effort data is recorded across the entire network. This protocol is now the backbone of the organisation’s fisheries monitoring programme.
Outcomes: - New protocol adopted across the Mwambao network - Standardised data collection improving consistency and data quality - Protocol supports catch trend analysis and stock assessment - Replaces and improves upon the previous monitoring framework
Tools: Protocol design · KoboToolbox · R · Stakeholder consultation
Protocol Development Fisheries Management Institutional Tools


Ecological Survey Forms — 10+ Tools Designed
Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
Designed and built 10+ digital and paper-based ecological survey forms covering all ecosystem types monitored by the Mwambao ecological department — including reef fish, coral cover, seagrass, mangrove, octopus, and prawn surveys. Forms are used by field teams and community monitors across all active sites.
Outcomes: - 10+ survey forms in active use across programmes - Consistent data structure across reef fish, coral, seagrass, mangrove, octopus, and prawn monitoring - Reduced field data errors and improved data comparability - Digital forms integrated with KoboToolbox for streamlined data flow
Tools: KoboToolbox · ODK · Excel · Field testing with monitors
Survey Design Data Management Multi-Ecosystem


Ecological Data Analysis — Full Department
Organisation: Mwambao Coastal Community Network
Responsible for all ecological data analysis across Mwambao’s ecological department — spanning fisheries, coral reef monitoring, mangrove restoration, and seagrass monitoring programmes. Built reproducible R-based analytical pipelines and produces annual reports and dashboards that drive conservation planning.
Outcomes: - End-to-end analysis across four major ecosystem monitoring programmes - Annual biodiversity and restoration reports produced for stakeholders - R pipelines automating data cleaning, analysis, and visualisation - Adaptive management recommendations delivered based on analytical findings
Tools: R · ggplot2 · Quarto · KoboToolbox API · QGIS · SQL
Data Analysis R Programming Multi-Programme Reporting

title: Documentaries & Media
text: Stories from the Swahili Coast — conservation through film and media

title: Documentaries & Media
text:
Documentary Features
My field work and conservation expertise have been featured in documentary productions highlighting the challenges and progress of coastal conservation in Tanzania. These films bring the science and the human stories of ocean stewardship to audiences worldwide.


Documentary Feature 1
Year: 2021 | Region: Swahili Coast, Tanzania
Description: This documentary explores community-based marine conservation along Tanzania’s coast, featuring ecological monitoring fieldwork and interviews with fishing communities. The film captures the intersection of traditional fishing knowledge and modern conservation science.
My Role: Field researcher and technical consultant — guided film crew through reef survey methodology and facilitated community interview sessions.

  ▶
  Video embed — add YouTube/Vimeo URL to activate
  


Marine Conservation Community Tanzania Field Research




Documentary Feature 2
Year: 2022 | Region: Pemba Island & Mainland Coast
Description: A closer look at the ecological health of Pemba Island’s coral reefs and the monitoring systems in place to track change. Features underwater footage of reef fish surveys, coral health assessments, and discussions of climate resilience.
My Role: Lead field researcher — coordinated all in-water survey activities and provided ecological commentary for the film’s scientific narrative.

  ▶
  Video embed — add YouTube/Vimeo URL to activate


Coral Reefs Biodiversity Pemba Island Climate




Short Film: Community Data Collectors
Year: 2023 | Format: Short documentary
Description: A short film spotlighting the community-based monitoring programme — following local fishers trained as citizen scientists as they conduct reef health surveys. A human-centred story about conservation ownership.
My Role: Programme designer and trainer for the citizen science programme featured in the film.

  ▶
  Video embed — add YouTube/Vimeo URL to activate


Citizen Science Community Training Short Film

title: Documentaries & Media
text:
Science Communication
Beyond documentary work, I communicate conservation science through:


📊 Data Reports
Annual and project-specific biodiversity reports translated from technical datasets into accessible findings for government, NGO, and community audiences.


🗣️ Workshops & Presentations
Presenting monitoring results at multi-stakeholder forums, including government planning sessions and conservation network convenings.


📱 Field Storytelling
Using photography and field notes to document and share conservation stories from monitoring sites — bridging the gap between fieldwork and public awareness.




  Have a documentary project or media opportunity? I'm open to collaborations that amplify conservation science.
